text.p: PAPER 2 (Drama and Poetry)
SECTION A (African Drama)
The city of Owu was a peaceful one. They were blessed economically by their gods. They flourished in their economic status and also they were peagans.

But the people of Owu wronged their gods which led to the destruction of their city. There was a prophecy that a son who would be bore by the queen Erelu would bring calamity but the queen refused. They were told to kill the child but the queen hid him till he grew up and became a full grown man. His name was Dejumo. A

A woman named Iyunoye was bethroded to Okunade who was good in the work of arts. They loved each other very much but Iyunoye was forcefully tookn her away from him and given to prince Dejumo. This made Okunade very sad and angry and made him sort a way to revenge on the people of Owu, so he left his work and joined the army.

The people of Owu also committed a sacralage by selling their fellow to slavery to the white men. It was a taboo for a Yoruba to sell a fellow yoruba. So, when the goddess Lawunmi saw this she was very angry and decided to punish the people of Owu for their sins. Anlugbua her son tried to plead on their behalf to this mother but the goddess Lawumi refused.
SECTION B (Non-African Drama)
No 6
Humour can be said to be what arises laugh in the minds of the audience.

Humour can be cited in the play where a swiss machinery known as Bluntschli came into the house room of a lady known as Raina through the shutters and threatened her life if she shouted to alert the members of her household but she was after a long conversation between the both of them Raina found out that Bluntschli who is a soldier that ought to put ammunitions in his gun rather put chocolate. This event made Raina call him the chocolate cream soldier.
SECTION C (African Poetry)
No 9
The poem is a very simple one.

Boy on a swing by Oswald Mtshali is a simple poem but is deceptive because of the strategy in which the poet wrote it. There are so many poetic devices in the poem e.g
Asonance repitition and so on which makes the poem deceptive.
PAPER 3 SECTION A (African prose)
TANO - Tano is the god to the people of Brenhoma. The people of Brenhoma gave him supplications and also worshipped him. They put their trust in Tano and also called him their eminent god.
When a woman known as Pokuwaa was barren she went to Tano because she believed he would give her a child and gave him offerings and worshipped him always.

KORAMOA - Koramoa is a very close friend to Pokuwaa and also has deep affection for Pokuwaa. Koramoa is the second wife to Koffi Dafo. Their relationship was right from their childhood. Koramo was the one who got married to Koffi Dafo after Pokuwaa and Koffi Dafo divorced. A She always consoled Pokuwaa because whenever Pokuwaa thought of her barreness.
SECTION B (Non-African Prose)
No 5

The playwright used symbolism in the novel Santiago (the old man) represents Jesus Christ when the boy which is Manolin told him his the holes wounds in his palms looked like fishless desert and they were so deep.
Manolin represents a faithful servant. He helped Santiago the old him man in his fishing work.
The sea represents the world and its troubles.
The marlin (fish) represents the difficulties Jesus went through when he was in the world.
